content: Stroke is classified as ischemic (87% of cases, caused by vessel occlusion) or hemorrhagic (13%, caused by bleeding). Ischemic stroke results from thrombotic or embolic occlusion; hemorrhagic from intracerebral or subarachnoid bleeding. The ischemic penumbra represents salvageable tissue surrounding the infarct core. Time-to-treatment is critical: 'time is brain' with approximately 1.9 million neurons lost per minute during large-vessel occlusion. tPA (alteplase/tenecteplase) window is 0-4.5 hours from symptom onset for eligible patients. Mechanical thrombectomy extends treatment window to 6-24 hours for large-vessel occlusions based on perfusion imaging (DAWN, DEFUSE-3 trials). Hemorrhagic stroke requires blood pressure control, reversal of anticoagulation, and possible neurosurgical intervention.
